headline: A compact benchmark harness for comparing Qwen target-only serving against DFlash speculative serving through OpenAI-compatible SGLang or vLLM endpoints.

runs.label: Baseline server
runs.notes: Launch target-only serving first, then run scripts/bench_openai_server.py against the OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

runs.label: DFlash server
runs.notes: DFlash is a packaged speculator tied to the target model. Use BF16-capable GPUs; T4 is not the intended hardware.

milestones.title: Prompt generation
milestones.body: scripts/make_prompts.py builds FineWeb-Edu prompt prefixes for repeatable benchmark inputs.

milestones.title: Target-only baseline
milestones.body: scripts/launch_baseline.sh starts SGLang or vLLM target-only serving.

milestones.title: DFlash launch
milestones.body: scripts/launch_dflash.sh starts target plus DFlash speculative serving.

milestones.title: OpenAI-compatible benchmark
milestones.body: scripts/bench_openai_server.py measures latency, TTFT, output tokens/sec, per-request status, and W&B artifacts.

milestones.title: Result comparison
milestones.body: scripts/compare_results.py compares baseline and DFlash JSON files and can log speedup ratios to W&B.

decisions: Keep raw result files under ignored results/; publish compact summaries in docs/data/summary.json.
Use SGLang as the recommended first DFlash serving path; keep vLLM DFlash marked experimental.
Use cheap profile defaults for RTX-style GPUs before increasing memory, concurrency, or backend aggression.
Compare baseline and DFlash through the same OpenAI-compatible client script and the same prompts.
